title: CPI April 3.8% — highest in 3 years, Iran war drives energy/gasoline (+28.4% YoY) prices
snippet: US CPI rose 3.8% YoY in April 2026, up from 3.3% in March — highest since May 2023. Energy prices accounted for 40% of total increase. Gasoline +28.4% YoY, airline fares +20.7%, beef +14.8%. Average hourly wages fell 0.3% after inflation — first YoY drop in 3 years. Iran war Hormuz closure directly responsible.
tag_reason: April 2026 CPI data released 5/12, not previously reported
